subject: Databases
title: Good Design vs Bad Design
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Good Design vs Bad Design.

hook.text: A startup stored users, orders, and payments in one giant table. Queries took 30 seconds by month six.

buildup.text: Bad design means duplicated data, slow queries, and bugs that are nearly impossible to fix later.

discovery.text: Good design separates concerns: one table per entity, clear relationships, enforced constraints.

twist.text: Fixing a bad schema in production means migrating millions of rows while the app keeps running.

climax.text: Spend time on design upfront. It's cheaper than rewriting the schema under a live product.

punchline.text: Design well now or redesign painfully later.

quiz.question: Why is fixing a bad database design in production so difficult?
options: You must migrate millions of rows while keeping the application running
Production databases cannot be altered at all
Bad designs always crash the server immediately
